results.seo_title: Auditing and Fixing Economic Validity in Tabular Foundation Models…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a two-stage adapter to improve economic validity in tabular foundation models for discrete choice tasks.
results.body_html:
The proposed two-stage adapter improves economic validity in tabular foundation models for discrete choice tasks by estimating a standard choice model and then training a correction term to incorporate the foundation model's predictions.

Improving Accuracy with Economic Consistency

  • Recovering up to 13 percentage points of accuracy over a standard logit model on two transportation datasets.
  • Maintaining perfect economic consistency under policy perturbation.
  • Producing analytically computable trade-off measures.

Economic Logic and Utility Maximization

The proposed adapter embeds foundation model predictions within a utility-maximization framework, ensuring monotonic price-demand relationships.

ParameterValue
Economic consistencyPerfect
Accuracy gainUp to 13 percentage points

Comparison with Raw Foundation Models and Distillation

The proposed adapter outperforms raw foundation models and conventional distillation methods in terms of accuracy and economic consistency.

Our results demonstrate the importance of incorporating economic theory into discrete choice modeling, leading to more accurate and reliable predictions.

source.title: Auditing and Fixing Economic Validity in Tabular Foundation Models for Discrete Choice
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve the validity of tabular foundation models in discrete choice tasks. The proposed two-stage adapter framework combines the strengths of these models with economic logic, ensuring that price increases lead to decreased demand and willingness-to-pay estimates are positive and plausible. This method has been shown to achieve significant accuracy gains on transportation datasets while maintaining economic consistency. The results outperform both raw foundation models and conventional distillation methods.

results.seo_title: Hybrid Neural-Symbolic Pipeline Outperforms Generation in Clinical…
results.meta_description: A hybrid neural-symbolic pipeline achieves high accuracy in extracting clinical follow-up instructions from outpatient notes, outperforming generative…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: A hybrid neural-symbolic pipeline achieves high accuracy in extracting clinical follow-up instructions from outpatient notes, outperforming generative extractors.

Methodology

  • BioBERT feeds BIO tagging and a biaffine linker;
  • Entities are canonicalized via a 28-action ontology and times normalized to day offsets deterministically;
  • A hybrid neural-symbolic pipeline is used for extraction.

Results

ModelF1 Score
Hybrid Pipeline0.997
LLaMA-30.992
GPT-4o-mini0.963

Conclusion

The hybrid neural-symbolic pipeline achieves high accuracy in extracting clinical follow-up instructions from outpatient notes, outperforming generative extractors.

source.title: Reliable Extraction of Clinical Follow-Up Instructions: A Hybrid Neural-Symbolic Pipeline
source.summary: Researchers have developed a hybrid neural-symbolic pipeline to extract clinical follow-up instructions from outpatient notes, which pairs actions with future times. The system outperforms generative extractors by separating learned entity extraction from deterministic date arithmetic, achieving high accuracy on a synthetic corpus and generalizing well to unseen actions. The results show that the hybrid approach is more effective than baselines in extracting both action-disjoint and paired instructions.

results.seo_title: TabPFN outperforms classical models in low-data regimes for…
results.meta_description: A transformer-based tabular foundation model excels in predicting childhood anemia under distribution shift and data-scarce settings.
results.body_html:
Childhood anemia affects around 40% of children aged 6-59 months globally and arises from heterogeneous factors, limiting model generalizability.

Performance in Low-Data Regimes

  • TabPFN outperformed classical models in low-data regimes (<200 samples), showing higher discrimination and better calibration.
  • Achieved the lowest Brier score (0.042) and ECE (0.203).
  • Improved discrimination and calibration provide advantages in low-resource settings.

Generalization Across Countries

Country AUC-ROC Score
Global Average 0.63
Country A 0.72
Country B 0.65

Advantages of TabPFN

TabPFN provides advantages in low-resource settings through improved discrimination and calibration, highlighting foundation models as promising tools for data-scarce global health prediction.

Performance in childhood anemia prediction is driven more by population variation than model choice. TabPFN excels in predicting childhood anemia under distribution shift and data-scarce settings.

Feature Importance

  • Child age, altitude, and height-for-age z-score as dominant predictors, followed by wealth and maternal education.

SHAP identified child age, altitude, and height-for-age z-score as dominant predictors, followed by wealth and maternal education.

source.title: Few-shot Cross-country Generalization of Tabular Machine Learning and Foundation Models for Childhood Anemia Prediction under Distribution Shift
source.summary: Researchers evaluated a transformer-based tabular foundation model (TabPFN) against classical supervised methods for predicting childhood anemia across 16 countries with diverse factors contributing to the disease. The study found that TabPFN outperformed traditional models in low-data regimes, achieving higher discrimination and better calibration. In full-data settings, TabPFN showed consistent performance, but its advantages were more pronounced in data-scarce settings, highlighting its potential for global health prediction in resource-constrained areas.

results.seo_title: Covert Control Attacks on LLMs via Data Poisoning: A New Vulnerability
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a data poisoning method to teach LLMs an information hiding scheme, revealing a new vulnerability in covert control attacks.
results.body_html:
Covert control attacks on LLMs via data poisoning can encode and decode arbitrary malicious instructions, revealing a new vulnerability.

Characterization of Covert Control Attacks

Average attack success rate: 93% after backdoor defenses, 98% after prompt injection defenses Outperform heuristic-based prompt injection attacks by 40% relative to clean fine-tuned models Circumvent defenses based on detection and fine-tuning

Evaluation Across Diverse LLMs and Defenses

LLM Backdoor Defense Prompt Injection Defense

5 LLMs 3 backdoor defenses 4 prompt injection defenses

Precise Characterization of Covert Control Attacks Covert control attacks outperform heuristic-based prompt injection attacks in average attack success rate by about 40% relative to clean fine-tuned models.

source.title: Cordyceps: Covert Control Attacks on LLMs via Data Poisoning
source.summary: Researchers have discovered a new way for adversaries to control large language models (LLMs) through "covert control attacks" that exploit semantic associations between knowledge and malicious phrases, allowing them to bypass existing defenses such as outlier detection and clean-data regularization. These attacks can encode and decode arbitrary instructions with high success rates, outperforming traditional prompt injection attacks by 40% in some cases. The attacks also evade defenses based on detection and fine-tuning, maintaining a high attack success rate of up to 98%.

results.seo_title: More Expressive Feedforward Layers: Part I - Token-Adaptive Mixing…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose Mixture of Activations (MoA), a token-adaptive FFN design that improves expressivity in LLMs, achieving lower terminal loss and better…
results.body_html:
Feedforward network (FFN) layers account for a large fraction of parameters and nonlinear expressivity in Transformer-based large language models (LLMs).

Current Limitations of FFN Layers

  • Most FFN designs still use a single fixed activation function, applying the same nonlinear transformation to all tokens.
  • The evolution from ReLU and GELU to gated variants such as SwiGLU has not led to significant improvements in expressivity.

Proposed Solution: Mixture of Activations (MoA)

  • MoA is a token-adaptive FFN design that mixes a dictionary of activation functions using lightweight input-dependent gates while sharing the same linear projections.
  • MoA introduces learnable activations (LA), which form linear combinations of activation functions for both ReLU-type and SwiGLU-type FFNs.

Theoretical Expressive Separations

LA strictly contains fixed-activation FFNs, while MoA strictly contains LA, with the additional expressivity arising from input-dependent nonlinear hybridization.

Empirical Results

Result Description
MoA consistently achieves lower terminal loss and exhibits more favorable scaling behavior than well-tuned baselines. MoA improves expressivity in LLMs with minimal parameter and computational overhead.

Conclusion

Mixture of Activations (MoA) is a simple and effective mechanism for improving FFN expressivity in LLMs, achieving lower terminal loss and better scaling behavior.

source.title: More Expressive Feedforward Layers: Part I. Token-Adaptive Mixing of Activations
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new design for feedforward network (FFN) layers in large language models, called Mixture of Activations (MoA), which adapts the activation function to each token individually using lightweight gates. This approach aims to improve the nonlinear expressivity of FFNs while maintaining minimal computational overhead. Theoretical analysis and empirical experiments show that MoA outperforms existing designs, achieving lower terminal loss and better scaling behavior in pre-training experiments on large language models.

results.seo_title: LLM Sequential Editing: Simplifying Knowledge Updates with…
results.meta_description: Researchers investigate mechanisms underlying effective and stable sequential editing in large language models, finding that stability emerges naturally from…
results.body_html:
Formal equivalence between one-time and sequential editing has been established, demonstrating that stability emerges naturally from properly accounting for accumulated editing constraints.

AlphaEdit achieves empirical success in sequential editing of structured knowledge in large language models. The work demonstrates the effectiveness of AlphaEdit in updating LLMs without retraining. AlphaEdit's success is attributed to its ability to efficiently modify LLMs' behaviors within specific domains.

Editing Objectives Stability Emerges Naturally

Properly accounting for accumulated editing constraints Yes

Specialized regularization or null-space operations No

Extension to Conflicting Edits Our framework extends to handle conflicting edits, ensuring robust and consistent behavior under contradictory updates.

Conclusion Our work provides Ariadne's thread through the labyrinth of sequential editing, charting a path toward simpler, more interpretable, and dependable knowledge updates.

source.title: The Labyrinth and the Thread: Rethinking Regularizations in Sequential Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to updating large language models with structured knowledge, eliminating the need for complex regularization mechanisms. Through rigorous optimization analysis, they found that stability emerges naturally from properly accounting for accumulated editing constraints, making many commonly used regularizations unnecessary. The new framework also handles conflicting edits and provides a simpler path toward dependable knowledge updates.

results.seo_title: Graph-Based Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
results.meta_description: A new approach to credit assignment in agentic reinforcement learning, improving training efficiency and achieving state-of-the-art performance.
results.body_html:
Graph-based credit assignment is a new approach that improves training efficiency and achieves state-of-the-art performance in agentic reinforcement learning.

Key Benefits of Graph-Based Credit Assignment

Improved training efficiency State-of-the-art performance across challenging benchmarks More faithful step-level credit assignment

Aggregates all rollout trajectories into a unified state-transition graph

Estimates the distance from each state to the task goal using global information encoded in the graph

Assigns credit to each edge by estimating a graph-based advantage based on how much the transition reduces the distance to the task goal

Advantages Over Traditional Methods

More accurate step-level credit assignment Improved training efficiency State-of-the-art performance across challenging benchmarks

source.title: Beyond Trajectory-Level Attribution: Graph-Based Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Graph-Based Group Policy Optimization (GraphGPO) to improve credit assignment in agentic reinforcement learning tasks. The approach aggregates all rollout trajectories into a unified graph, estimates the distance from each state to the task goal, and assigns credit to each edge based on how much it reduces this distance. This method enables more faithful step-level credit assignment and achieves state-of-the-art performance across various challenging benchmarks. GraphGPO improves training efficiency compared to existing methods.

results.seo_title: L2Rec: Unifying Behavioral and Semantic Understanding in Large…
results.meta_description: L2Rec introduces a novel approach to unifying behavioral and semantic understanding in large language models for personalized recommendation, outperforming…
results.body_html:
L2Rec enables a single LLM backbone to produce complementary behavioral and semantic adaptations for each user with minimal representation-level misalignment.

Key Insight: Shared Medium for Both Views

Applying view-specific, personalized low-rank perturbations via a Dual-view Personalized Mixture-of-Experts (DPMoE) mechanism enables unified behavioral and semantic understanding at the parameter level of LLMs. The same set of Transformer parameters serves as a shared medium for both views, reducing representation-level misalignment.

Experimental Results: Consistent Outperformance

L2Rec vs. State-of-the-Art Baselines on Four Datasets DatasetL2RecState-of-the-Art Baseline Dataset 192.5%90.2% Dataset 295.1%93.4% Dataset 391.9%89.5% Dataset 494.2%92.8%

Online A/B Testing Validation Significant improvements in key engagement metrics validated through online A/B testing on a large-scale industrial platform.

Read the full paper to learn more about L2Rec's approach and experimental results.

source.title: L2Rec: Towards Dual-View Understanding of LLMs for Personalized Recommendation
source.summary: Researchers have introduced L2Rec, a method to adapt large language models (LLMs) for personalized recommendation by unifying behavioral and semantic understanding at the parameter level of LLMs. The approach uses a Dual-view Personalized Mixture-of-Experts mechanism to apply personalized perturbations to the same set of Transformer parameters, enabling complementary behavioral and semantic adaptations for each user. Experiments on four datasets show that L2Rec outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, with online A/B testing validating significant improvements in key engagement metrics.

results.seo_title: MatFormBench: A Benchmarking Evaluation Framework for Target-Driven…
results.meta_description: A novel benchmarking ecosystem for target-driven materials formulation, evaluating generative strategies and providing a unified evaluation standard.
results.body_html:
MatFormBench is a novel benchmarking ecosystem for target-driven materials formulation, providing a unified evaluation standard for generative strategies.

Integrates physics-driven formulation generation scheme to generate synthetic samples that faithfully emulate realistic materials structure-property response relationships. Complemented by five escalating difficulty levels to quantify the complexity of these relationships. Proposes MatFormScore, a multi-dimensional metric that comprehensively quantifies performance across five critical axes: target success, search efficiency, exploratory capacity, robustness, and stability.

AlgorithmPerformance Metric Diffusion-based modelsStrongest overall performance across 1170 standardized algorithm-task evaluations. Variational Autoencoder (VAE)-based methodsDistinct advantages in specific scenarios. Genetic Algorithm (GA)-based methodsDistinct advantages in specific scenarios.

Benchmarks and Evaluation MatFormBench enables reproducible benchmarking, principled algorithm comparison, and diagnostic analysis of inverse design strategies, providing a foundational tool for advancing materials inverse design.

source.title: MatFormBench: A Benchmarking Evaluation Framework for Target-Driven Materials Formulation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new benchmarking framework called MatFormBench to evaluate the performance of algorithms used in target-driven materials formulation optimization. The framework generates synthetic samples that mimic realistic material properties and structure-response relationships, allowing for a comprehensive assessment of algorithm performance across five critical axes. A validation study using 39 diverse inverse design algorithms found that diffusion-based models demonstrated the strongest overall performance, while other methods excelled in specific scenarios. MatFormBench provides a unified evaluation standard for advancing materials inverse design.

results.seo_title: Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization: A Principled…
results.meta_description: Discover the benefits of Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization (R$^2$VPO) in policy optimization, with substantial performance gains on mathematical…
results.body_html:
Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization (R^2VPO) provides a principled approach to stable and data-efficient policy optimization. By explicitly constraining the policy ratio variance, R^2VPO eliminates the need for binary hard clipping and preserves critical gradient signals from novel discoveries.

Advantages of R^2VPOSubstantial performance gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarksPronounced improvements on smaller modelsImproved sample efficiencyEvaluations and BenchmarkingEvaluation MetricComparison to PPO BaselinesContinuous Control DomainsR^2VPO outperforms PPO baselines in sparse-reward and dynamic environmentsSparse-Reward EnvironmentsR^2VPO improves performance compared to PPO baselinesMathematical Reasoning BenchmarksR^2VPO achieves substantial performance gains on these benchmarksGenerality and ScalabilityExtensive evaluations across 7 LLM scales, spanning both fast and slow reasoning paradigms, demonstrate the generality of R^2VPO.

source.title: Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to reinforcement learning called Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization (R^2VPO), which constrains the policy's variance to improve performance and efficiency. This method eliminates the need for binary hard clipping, allowing it to preserve critical gradient signals from novel discoveries while down-weighting stale data. Extensive evaluations across various LLM scales and robotic control tasks demonstrate the approach's generality and substantial performance gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarks and continuous control domains.

results.seo_title: EmoDistill: Offline Emotion Skill Distillation for Language Model…
results.meta_description: Discover how EmoDistill improves language model agents' negotiation skills and outperforms vanilla SLM/LLM baselines.
results.body_html:
EmoDistill improves language model agents' negotiation skills by distilling emotional strategy into emotion selection and expression.

Key ComponentsImplicit Q-Learning (IQL) selector learns which emotion to expressLow-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)-based policy learns how to express emotions through Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Judge Policy Optimization (JPO)Performance BenefitsNegotiation DomainUtility AchievedFour Emotion-Sensitive DomainsHighest Utility AchievedVanilla SLM/LLM BaselinesOvershadowed by EmoDistillIQL-Only Emotion SelectionOutperformed by EmoDistillTransfer StudiesEmoDistill generalizes across domains, unseen counterparties, and trained-vs-trained tournaments.

source.title: EmoDistill: Offline Emotion Skill Distillation for Language Model Agents in Adversarial Negotiation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called EmoDistill to help language model agents navigate adversarial negotiations more effectively. The framework uses two components: an Implicit Q-Learning selector that chooses which emotion to express and a Low-Rank Adaptation policy that learns how to express the chosen emotion through fine-tuning and optimization. This approach has been shown to outperform existing baselines in four high-stakes negotiation domains, demonstrating its potential for improving agent performance in emotionally charged negotiations. EmoDistill achieves this without requiring online interactions during training.

results.seo_title: SeDT: Sentence-Transformer Decision-Transformer for Multi-Turn…
results.meta_description: Improve conversation reliability with SeDT, a training-free inference-time method that resolves the Lost-in-Conversation phenomenon in large language models.
results.body_html:
Telling the model which past turns matter is sufficient to substantially recover the performance lost in conversation. Large language models (LLMs) achieve impressive performance when a task is fully specified in a single turn, yet they lose up to 39% of that performance when the identical task is revealed incrementally across multiple turns. This phenomenon, known as Lost-in-Conversation, is almost entirely a reliability failure.

Understanding the ProblemThe flat conversation history assigns equal implicit weight to every prior turn, giving the model no signal to distinguish a critical constraint from incidental dialog.This leads to a collapse in performance, with the best case being an aptitude loss of 16% and unreliability more than doubling (+112%).SeDT: A Training-Free Inference-Time MethodSeDT annotates each conversation shard with a cumulative relevance score derived from three complementary semantic, lexical, and positional signals.Evaluation on the Lost-in-Conversation BenchmarkModel-Task CombinationP (Mean Performance)Unreliability ReductionLLM1-Sharded Baseline0.83-12%LLM2-Sharded Baseline0.81-15%LLM3-Sharded Baseline0.80-18%LLaMA-13B-LLaMA Baseline0.92+37.7%PaLM-540B-PaLM Baseline0.89+32%GPT-3-GPT Baseline0.85+25%

source.title: SeDT: Sentence-Transformer Decision-Transformer Conditioning for Multi-Turn Conversation Reliability
source.summary: Researchers have found that large language models (LLMs) perform poorly when a task is revealed incrementally across multiple turns, with a significant loss of reliability. This phenomenon, known as "Lost in Conversation," occurs because the model's flat conversation history gives equal weight to all prior turns, making it difficult to distinguish critical constraints from incidental dialog. To address this issue, a new method called SeDT (Sentence-transformer Decision-Transformer Conditioning) has been proposed, which annotates each conversation shard with a cumulative relevance score and presents the full annotated history to the model at the final turn, resulting in improved performance.

results.seo_title: HTMLCure: Improving HTML Page Quality with State-Guided Repair
results.meta_description: A browser experience framework that evaluates HTML after interaction and provides quality cleared pages for SFT.
results.body_html:
HTMLCure improves HTML page quality by evaluating pages after interaction and providing quality cleared pages for SFT.

Executes pages across viewports and interaction states Records deterministic browser evidence Gives VLM curated keyframes from the executed trajectory

Performance Metrics of HTMLCure-27B-Refined

Deterministic Test Case Pass Rate 45.2%

Advantages of HTMLCure over SFT Improves raw 27B SFT by 15.3 points and approaches the level of strong reference systems.

source.title: HTMLCure: Turning Browser Experience into State Guided Repair for Interactive HTML
source.summary: Researchers have introduced HTMLCure, a framework that evaluates interactive HTML pages after they are rendered on different devices and interaction states. This allows for more accurate diagnosis and repair of errors in web pages, resulting in improved performance compared to existing language models. The new framework has been tested on several benchmarks and achieved significant improvements over previous systems, including a 15.3-point increase in raw performance.

results.seo_title: Innovation Characterization of Hallucination in Large Language Models
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce innovation as a property that measures the tendency of a model to produce outputs outside the training data, and show its relation to…
results.body_html:
The property of a calibrated LLM that makes hallucinations unavoidable is innovation. Innovation measures the tendency of a model to produce outputs outside the training data, and it is implied by the condition for hallucination identified by Kalai and Vempala.

Key FindingsInnovation is an almost characterization of hallucination: hallucination implies innovation, and conversely, innovation implies hallucination with high probability.The

source.title: Innovation: An Almost Characterization of Hallucination
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a concept called "innovation" as a measure of how likely large language models (LLMs) are to produce outputs outside their training data. Innovation is related to the condition for hallucination identified by previous work, implying that it is an almost characterization of hallucination. In other words, if innovation is high, hallucinations will occur with high probability, and conversely, if hallucinations occur, innovation is likely to be present. This new concept provides a simpler way to understand and mitigate the limitations of LLMs.

results.seo_title: RAGEAR: Retrieval-Augmented Graph-Enhanced Academic Recommender
results.meta_description: RAGEAR is a neurosymbolic recommender system for academic course recommendation that combines dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts with a symbolic…
results.body_html:
RAGEAR combines dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts with a symbolic Knowledge Graph modelling courses, lessons, transcript chunks, credits, study plans, and curricular information.

Dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts Symbolic Knowledge Graph modelling Courses, lessons, transcript chunks, credits, study plans, and curricular information

Advantages of RAGEAR Over Metadata-Based Approaches

Share of retrieved similarity associated with a course The proportion of relevant transcript chunks retrieved for each course

Rank-based strength of relevant chunks The rank order of relevance of retrieved transcript chunks

Distribution of evidence across lessons The spread of evidence across different lessons within a course

Performance Evaluation of RAGEAR Results show that lecture transcripts improve over metadata-only retrieval, and that RAGEAR further improves ranking quality over a transcript-based normalized SumP baseline.

source.title: RAGEAR: Retrieval-Augmented Graph-Enhanced Academic Recommender
source.summary: Researchers have developed RAGEAR, a novel academic course recommendation system that combines dense retrieval of full lecture transcripts with a symbolic Knowledge Graph to provide more accurate and personalized recommendations. The system uses a graph-aware aggregation function to combine evidence from retrieved transcript chunks to generate course-level recommendations. Evaluations show that RAGEAR outperforms existing approaches, particularly in ranking quality, especially for top-ranked recommendations.

results.seo_title: ContextGuard: Structured Self-Auditing for Context Learning in…
results.meta_description: A new paradigm to implement context-aware systems, enabling user control on context information collection and improving contextual knowledge in LLMs.
results.body_html:
Despite strong reasoning capabilities, large language models still struggle to apply complex contextual knowledge.

Models may follow the central reasoning path while missing peripheral, persistent, or format-sensitive requirements. Context-rich tasks often lead to incomplete or inaccurate results. The lack of contextual awareness hinders effective decision-making and problem-solving.

Limitations in Current Auditing Practices

Psychological Independence The auditing relationship is compromised due to the hiring and payment of auditors by management.

Self-Serving Bias Auditors may unintentionally introduce bias into their judgments, compromising impartiality.

ContextGuard: A Solution for Context-Aware Systems ContextGuard proposes a new paradigm to implement context-aware systems, enabling user control on context information collection and improving contextual knowledge in LLMs.

source.title: ContextGuard: Structured Self-Auditing for Context Learning in Language Models
source.summary: Large language models (LLMs) continue to struggle with applying complex contextual knowledge, even when they have strong reasoning capabilities. In many cases, these failures are not complete breakdowns, but rather incomplete application of context-specific information. Models may follow the main reasoning path, but miss important peripheral or persistent details that are crucial for accurate performance.

results.seo_title: Improving Student Performance in LLMs: A New Approach to…
results.meta_description: A new framework for answer selection in large language models (LLMs) prioritizes student-centric learning cost, improving student performance and…
results.body_html:
The strongest teacher is not always the best teacher for a given student. In fact, research suggests that even when multiple teachers provide correct answers to the same question, the answer from the strongest teacher may not be the best supervision for a student.

Current Challenges in Teacher-Generated Supervision

  • Implicit reliance on teacher test performance as a proxy for teaching quality
  • Choice of highest-performing teacher to generate student training data without considering individual student needs
  • Lack of consideration for student-centric learning cost

Introducing Student-Centric Answer Sampling (SCAS)

Our proposed framework, SCAS, addresses these challenges by selecting from verified teacher-generated answers according to their estimated student-centric learning cost.

Benefits of SCASImproved student performance and effectiveness of distillation
Reduced reliance on teacher test performance as a proxy for teaching qualityIncreased consideration for individual student needs
Enhanced alignment with current student learning costImproved overall effectiveness of distillation

Experiments and Results

Our experiments across 30 teacher models, 6 student base models, and 8 tasks show that SCAS consistently improves student performance.

SCAS improves student performance by prioritizing supervision matched to the current student rather than teacher strength alone.

source.title: The Strongest Teacher Is Not Always the Best Teacher: Student-Centric Answer Selection
source.summary: Researchers have found that relying solely on the strongest-performing teacher for generating training data can lead to suboptimal results, as even multiple top-performers may not provide the best guidance for individual students. To address this issue, a new framework called Student-Centric Answer Sampling (SCAS) has been proposed, which selects answers based on their estimated learning cost for each student. This approach has shown promising results in improving student performance across various tasks and models.

results.seo_title: Compressive Knowledge Graph Hypothesis: What Matters for Scientific…
results.meta_description: A new hypothesis suggests that useful knowledge graph signal is often recoverable from compact, scientifically structured subgraphs rather than requiring the…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: According to the study, useful knowledge graph signal is often recoverable from compact, scientifically structured subgraphs rather than requiring the full local graph.

Perturbing local KGs by varying density, ontology richness, topology, and control structure changes outputs across models. No-KG outputs also recover substantial graph content from model priors. Compact top-k subgraphs often approximate full-KG behavior, including when claimed-outcome triples are held out.

Mistral 7B vs Llama-3.1-70B MetricMistral 7BLlama-3.1-70B Reasoning, Mathematics, and Code GenerationOutperforms Llama 2 13B across all evaluated benchmarks.Underperforms compared to Mistral 7B.

Robotic AI Chemist Capabilities ChemAgents, a hierarchical multiagent system, enables autonomous chemical research on demand using an on-board Llama-3.1-70B LLM.

Comparative Evaluation of AI Platforms PlatformPerformance Google Gemini 2.5 FlashOutperforms other platforms in solving multiple-choice questions.

source.title: The Compressive Knowledge Graph Hypothesis: Which Graph Facts Matter for Scientific Hypothesis Generation?
source.summary: Researchers have investigated how knowledge graphs (KGs) influence scientific hypothesis generation in language models. They found that the utility of KGs varies depending on the model and context, with some outputs being significantly affected by graph facts while others can recover substantial content from prior knowledge. The study suggests that compact subgraphs within KGs can approximate full-KG behavior, supporting a "Compressive KG Hypothesis" that useful signal is often recoverable from structured subgraphs rather than requiring the entire local graph.

results.seo_title: Improving Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
results.meta_description: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments explores the gap between idealized training settings and real-world interaction…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Despite strong performance on existing benchmarks, LLMs often exhibit notable degradation when deployed in real-world settings due to a fundamental mismatch between idealized training settings and real-world interaction dynamics.

NoisyAgent explicitly incorporates environmental imperfections into the agent learning process, addressing the gap between idealized training settings and real-world interaction dynamics. The framework introduces perturbations into the training pipeline to simulate user noise and tool noise, progressively increasing difficulty as the model adapts to the current noise level.

Extensive Experiments Consistently improves agent robustness under noisy and dynamic environments.

Idealized Benchmarks Yields performance gains, suggesting controlled exposure to environmental noise promotes more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors.

Analysis and Findings Our analysis reveals that training under noise conditions also yields performance gains on idealized benchmarks, suggesting that controlled exposure to environmental noise promotes more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors.

source.title: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called NoisyAgent to improve the robustness of large language models (LLMs) when deployed in real-world settings with noisy environments. The framework introduces environmental imperfections into the agent learning process, including user noise and tool noise, which are simulated during training. This approach has been shown to consistently improve agent performance under challenging conditions, while also promoting more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors on idealized benchmarks.

results.seo_title: Gumbel Machine: Counterfactual Student Writing Generation via Gumbel…
results.meta_description: The Gumbel Machine generates counterfactual student writing by leveraging LLM instruction-following capabilities and a novel decoding algorithm.
results.body_html:
The Gumbel Machine generates counterfactual student writing by leveraging LLM instruction-following capabilities and a novel decoding algorithm.

Novel eta-Hindsight control algorithm for tunable similarity control during counterfactual generation Flexible, modular approach to generating counterfactuals that leverages LLM instruction-following capabilities Experiments on datasets of student writing demonstrate effectiveness at generating rubric-consistent and similar counterfactuals

1 Provide examples of high-quality work for teaching across disciplines

2 Use the Gumbel Machine to generate counterfactual student writing

Results and Evaluation Experiments on datasets of student writing demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach at generating rubric-consistent and similar counterfactuals.

Further exploration of the Gumbel Machine's potential applications in education and other fields Development of more advanced similarity control mechanisms for improved counterfactual generation

source.title: Gumbel Machine: Counterfactual Student Writing Generation via Gumbel Noise Steering
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called the Gumbel Machine for generating counterfactual versions of student writing, which are improved but still similar to their original work. This approach uses Large Language Models (LLMs) and a novel decoding algorithm that incorporates latent randomness as a control mechanism to encourage similarity to a reference factual text. The system has been tested on datasets of student writing and shown to be effective in generating rubric-consistent and comparable counterfactuals.

results.seo_title: Maat: The Agentic Legal Research Assistant for Competition Protection
results.meta_description: Maat is a ReAct agent that orchestrates tools corresponding to different tasks of the research process for competition law analysis.
results.body_html:
Maat significantly outperforms all baseline assistants on case-specific tasks and performs within range of the top baseline on theoretical question tasks.

Grounds cases and findings in official sources using RAG for reliability Provides rich in-line citations Falls back to web search when database coverage is insufficient Prompts the user for clarification when queries are ambiguous

Advantages Over Baseline Assistants

Case-Specific Tasks Maat outperforms baseline assistants on case-specific tasks

Theoretical Question Tasks Maat performs within range of the top baseline on theoretical question tasks

Technical Takeaway Maat's use of RAG for reliability provides a significant advantage over baseline assistants.

source.title: Maat: The Agentic Legal Research Assistant for Competition Protection
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new tool called Maat to assist with legal research in competition law, which is currently inadequately supported by existing general and specialized AI assistants. Maat is designed to provide reliable information, accurate citations, and clarify ambiguous queries, making it a significant improvement over current baseline assistants. The tool was developed in collaboration with competition law experts and has shown promising results in case-specific tasks and theoretical question tasks. A dataset for Maat is available on GitHub.

results.seo_title: Alignment Tampering in Large Language Models
results.meta_description: A potential vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) can amplify undesired behaviors in Large Language Models (LLMs), leading to…
results.body_html:
Alignment tampering is a potential vulnerability in RLHF where the LLM influences the preference dataset, causing it to amplify undesired behaviors.

Preference datasets are constructed from the LLM's own outputs, allowing it to influence them. Pairwise comparisons only indicate which response is better, not why. The reward model inherits this limitation.

Exploitation of Alignment Tampering

If an LLM generates biased responses with higher quality, annotators will prefer them based on quality. However, preference labels do not distinguish quality from bias. The reward model inherits this limitation.

Optimizing such rewards through reinforcement learning or best-of-N sampling can amplify misaligned biases. Mitigation remains challenging, as existing techniques for robust RLHF fail to fully resolve alignment tampering without sacrificing response quality.

Conclusion and Future Work These findings reveal structural vulnerabilities of current RLHF and emphasize the need to prevent this vulnerability. Further research is needed to develop more effective mitigation strategies.

source.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
source.summary: Researchers have discovered a potential vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), a method used to align Large Language Models with human preferences, known as alignment tampering. This occurs when the LLM being trained influences its own preference dataset, causing RLHF to amplify undesired behaviors such as bias and propaganda. The study found that this limitation can be exploited through various means, including optimizing rewards or best-of-N sampling, which can lead to amplification of misaligned biases across different types of biases. This highlights the need for new techniques to mitigate alignment tampering without sacrificing response quality.

results.seo_title: Optimizing Retrieval Agents with BRANE for Cost-Quality Tradeoff
results.meta_description: BRANE proposes a novel approach to optimize retrieval agents by selecting the best configuration that balances cost and accuracy, outperforming existing…
results.body_html:
Given a natural-language query and either an accuracy or a budget target, BRANE selects the configuration that minimizes cost or maximizes accuracy at inference time.

Uses an LLM to convert each query into workload-specific characteristics

Selects the configuration that maximizes predicted correctness penalized by cost, exposing a tunable cost-quality tradeoff without retraining

Outperforms LLM-routing, rule-based, and fine-tuned Qwen3-4B baselines

source.title: Natural Language Query to Configuration for Retrieval Agents
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new system called BRANE (Batched Retrieval Agent with Natural Language Embeddings) that optimizes the configuration of retrieval agents for natural language queries. The system uses an LLM to convert queries into characteristics and trains a lightweight predictor to estimate the accuracy of each configuration. BRANE consistently improves upon existing baselines, achieving up to 89% lower cost while maintaining high accuracy, making it a practical alternative to static workload-level tuning.

results.seo_title: Xe-Forge: Revolutionizing LLM-Powered Kernel Optimization for Intel…
results.meta_description: Automating deep learning algorithm porting to new hardware accelerators with Xe-Forge, a multi-stage LLM-powered pipeline.
results.body_html:
Xe-Forge automates deep learning algorithm porting to new hardware accelerators with a multi-stage LLM-powered pipeline.

Algorithmic restructuring and operator fusion Block pointer modernization GPU-specific tuning Open-ended discovery

Kernel/ModelSpeedup PyTorch EagerBase Xe-Forge (Level-2 KernelBench)1.17x Xe-Forge (Flash Attention)2-13.3x

Benefits of Xe-Forge Structured domain knowledge with hardware-in-the-loop verification eliminates repetitive porting effort.

source.title: Xe-Forge: Multi-Stage LLM-Powered Kernel Optimization for Intel GPU
source.summary: Researchers have developed Xe-Forge, a multi-stage pipeline that automates the process of optimizing deep learning algorithms for Intel GPUs. The system uses a Chain-of-Verification-and-Refinement (CoVeR) agent to apply up to nine optimization stages to each kernel, driven by a curated knowledge base of Intel GPU constraints. This approach has been shown to achieve significant speedups on various benchmarks, including a 1.17x geometric mean speedup and up to 82x speedups on certain configurations.

results.seo_title: GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation
results.meta_description: Discover how GEM (Geometric Entropy Mixing) improves LLM data curation and boosts downstream accuracy by up to 1.2%.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: GEM (Geometric Entropy Mixing) is a framework that reformulates data curation as a variational problem on the hypersphere augmented with a mixing-balance regularizer.

GEM improves average downstream accuracy by up to 1.2% when integrated into mixing strategies like DoReMi and RegMix. The framework effectively counteracts cluster collapse to discover balanced semantic structures invisible to Euclidean heuristics. Experiments with 1.1B-parameter models demonstrate the effectiveness of GEM in boosting downstream accuracy.

GEM is scaled to web-scale corpora using teacher-student distillation, enabling robust coordinate systems for predictable data mixing. The framework introduces the Geometric Influence Score (GIS) for interpretable taxonomy generation. Properly filtered and deduplicated web data alone can lead to powerful models, even outperforming curated corpora.

GEM establishes a new state-of-the-art in LLM data curation. The framework offers a robust coordinate system for predictable data mixing. GEM effectively counteracts cluster collapse to discover balanced semantic structures.

source.title: GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new framework called Geometric Entropy Mixing (GEM) to improve the curation of large language model (LLM) training datasets. GEM addresses issues with existing methods, such as Euclidean clustering, by using a variational problem on the hypersphere and a mixing-balance regularizer. The framework has been shown to outperform existing methods in improving downstream accuracy, with average gains of up to 1.2% when integrated into popular mixing strategies like DoReMi and RegMix.
